headlineSummary: Gemini skews negative on capability and trust concerns [E1][E3], Copilot is viewed positively for usefulness and integration [E2], and Claude and OpenAI sit in a close tie with mixed-neutral sentiment from limited evidence [E1].

ranking.summary: Gemini draws negative sentiment because commenters describe a regression in coding ability and criticize its sourcing as potentially harming trust [E1][E3].
Anthropic is viewed slightly positively in a close tie with OpenAI, based on limited evidence that praises Claude as being "so good at it" [E1].
OpenAI is viewed slightly positively in a close tie with Anthropic, based on limited evidence that groups ChatGPT with Claude as "so good at it" [E1].
Microsoft Copilot is viewed positively, with praise for doing a great job, integrating well with PR comments, and being cheap to use [E2].

evidence.excerpt: Has Gemini lost its ability to run javascript and python? I swear it could when it was launched by now its saying it hasn't the ability. Annoying regression when Claude and ChatGPT are so good at it.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Gemini lost a capability and calls it an "Annoying regression," which is direct criticism of its current capability.
Claude is explicitly praised in comparison as being "so good at it," indicating favorable sentiment about its capability.
ChatGPT is explicitly grouped with Claude as "so good at it," indicating favorable sentiment about its capability.

evidence.excerpt: We have GH copilot natively available on all our PRs, it does a great job, integrates very well with the PR comment system, and is cheap (free with our current usage patterns).

annotations.rationale: "GH copilot" clearly refers to GitHub Copilot, and the comment strongly praises its performance, integration, and cost.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini cites lots of "AI generated" videos as its primary source, which creates a closed loop and has the potential to debase shared reality.

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly criticizes Gemini's sourcing behavior as potentially harmful to reliability and shared reality, expressing strong distrust.
